name: Rendering Redesign: Data Mutation + Reactive Rendering

content.description:
# Rendering Redesign: Data Mutation + Reactive Rendering

## Core Principle

Code should only mutate data. Rendering handles itself reactively. Every API call must express its intent explicitly through its arguments ‚Äî no hidden context, no implicit fallback chains, no closure-captured state.

## The Problem Today

`renderItem` creates an `api` object per render instance with behavior baked in via closure over a `context` bag (`siblingContainer`, `parentId`, `decorator`, etc.). This causes:

- **Hidden magic.** `api.openItem(id)` decides where the item goes based on invisible captured context. The caller can't see or control the decision.
- **Fragile threading.** The `siblingContainer` object must be threaded through render contexts ‚Äî a Rube Goldberg chain where each layer must pass it correctly or the whole thing breaks.
- **Violates self-revealing principle.** Reading `api.openItem(id)` at a call site tells you nothing about what will happen. Is it adding a sibling? Navigating? Depends on context you can't see.
- **Concrete bug.** Items added via Ctrl+K search or Item Search on the canvas aren't draggable until page refresh, because the `siblingContainer` threading breaks somewhere in the chain.

## The Target Design

**The only action is data mutation. Rendering is reactive.**

To add an item to a canvas, you just modify the canvas item's attachments:

```
api.attach(parentId, { id: targetId, view: { x: 50, y: 50 } })
```

That's it. The reactive rendering system already watches for item changes. The canvas view already re-renders when its structural selector detects attachment changes. The new window appears with drag handlers because that's what the canvas view does when it renders its children.

The attachment object is plain data ‚Äî `id` plus an optional `view` bag with position, size, view type, etc. The caller describes what it wants; the canvas interprets it during rendering.

### What disappears

- **`siblingContainer`** ‚Äî gone. No capability object threaded through contexts. The child just calls `api.attach(parentId, attachment)`.
- **`openItem`** ‚Äî gone. It was a decision function ("figure out where to put this") that hid its logic. Replace with explicit `attach` (add to parent) or `navigate` (go there).
- **`addSibling`** ‚Äî gone. It was the siblingContainer's method. Now it's just `attach`.
- **Context-captured behavior** ‚Äî the `api` no longer carries implicit powers based on where it was created.

### What the child needs

Just `parentId` ‚Äî plain data, already inherent to the tree structure. A child that wants to add a sibling says:

```
;; Hob
(attach! parent-id {:id target-id :view {:x 50 :y 50}})

;; or just the id, let the canvas decide position
(attach! parent-id target-id)
```

### What the canvas does

Nothing new. It already works this way for its own re-rendering. When attachments change, the structural selector fires, `create-window` runs for each child, drag handlers are set up. The only change is that external callers (note links, search results, Ctrl+K) now use the same path.

## Transition Steps

### Step 1: Make `attach` accept full attachment data

Currently `api.attach(parentId, childId)` only takes an ID. Extend it to accept `{ id, view }` so callers can specify position/view.

### Step 2: Replace `open-item!` call sites

In each view that currently calls `open-item!` or `api.openItem`:
- If inside a canvas child (has `parentId`): replace with `(attach! parent-id target-id)`
- If at root level (Ctrl+K, REPL): replace with `(attach! (get-current-root) target-id)` or `(navigate! target-id)` depending on whether the root is a canvas

Key call sites:
- `a1b2c3d4` (markdown view) ‚Äî note link clicks
- `9428203f` (item-search-view) ‚Äî search result clicks
- `8e2f3e95` (note content view) ‚Äî item ref clicks
- `f1111111-0005` (item-palette) ‚Äî Ctrl+K
- `2ce4cf7d` (tag pill view) ‚Äî tag clicks
- `d0d0d0d0-0002` (inspector) ‚Äî various actions
- `ef977316` ‚Äî edit actions
- `b0dd6871` ‚Äî item ref navigation

### Step 3: Remove `siblingContainer` from the rendering pipeline

- Remove `siblingContainer` from `createAPI`'s context
- Remove `siblingContainer` from `renderItem`'s options/merging
- Remove `siblingContainer` from the canvas's `create-window` render call (no longer needed ‚Äî children use `attach` with explicit parent ID)
- Remove `get-sibling-container` from Hob runtime
- Remove `openItem` from the API

### Step 4: Clean up `item-palette` / Ctrl+K

With the new model, item-palette just needs to:
1. Show the search UI
2. On result click: `api.attach(rootId, { id: selectedId, view: { x, y } })` then hide

No siblingContainer creation, no threading, no special rendering context.

## Design Principle (recorded)

Every API call must express its intent explicitly through its arguments. If a call site can't be read and understood in isolation, the API is wrong.

- Bad: `api.openItem(id)` ‚Äî hides the "where" decision inside captured context
- Good: `api.attach(parentId, { id: targetId, view: { x: 50, y: 50 } })` ‚Äî says exactly what it does
